center.name: 马德雷德迪奥斯大区
center.summary: 马德雷德迪奥斯大区是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联17种鸟类，包括Fiery-capped manakin、Purple-throated cotinga、Marble-faced bristle tyrant等17种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In Peru it occurs in the departments of Ucayali, Cuzco, Madre de Dios, and Puno

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The brownish-headed antbird is found in the southeastern Peruvian departments of Cuzco, Madre de Dios, and Puno and in west-central La Paz Department in far west-central Bolivia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: One population is found in far eastern Peru's Department of Madre de Dios, northern and eastern Bolivia, and Brazil's western Amazon Basin

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in eastern Peru from eastern Loreto Department south to central Madre de Dios and in western Brazil's Amazonas and Acre states

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Its principal range in that country is in the southeast in Ucayali and Madre de Dios.{{cite book | last =Schulenberg | first =T.S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate subspecies of the flammulated bamboo tyrant is found in eastern Peru between San Martín and Madre de Dios departments, east into far western Brazil's Acre and Rondônia states, and south and east into northern Bolivia to Beni and Cochabamba departments

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: sororia is found in Peru south of the Marañón as far as the Department of Madre de Dios

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Humaita antbird is found in western Amazonian Brazil, the eastern part of the Department of Madre de Dios in southeastern Peru, and in Pando Department in far northern Bolivia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: ottonis is found from Cuzco and Madre de Dios departments in southeastern Peru south into Bolivia as far as Santa Cruz Department. The marble-faced bristle tyrant inhabits humid montane forest in the subtropical zone

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It also occurs in isolated populations in Caquetá and Vaupés departments in southeastern Colombia, in Cuzco and Madre de Dios departments in southeastern Peru, in La Paz and Beni departments in northern Bolivia, and in Rondônia state in western Amazonian Brazil

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|right|Rainforest canopy in Brazil The purple-throated cotinga is found throughout western Amazonia from southern Colombia south through eastern Ecuador and Peru to the Madre de Dios region and east through extreme northwestern Bolivia and into western Amazonian Brazil. The eastern boundary of the species appears to be the lower Rio Negro and northern Mato Grosso in Brazil. The purple-throated cotinga's total range covers approximately , throughout which it occurs in patches at low population densities. In southeastern Peru only two birds were found per , a ratio which likely is true across its range. The cotinga can be found in the canopy or borders of humid forest up to in elevation, but mostly is restricted to humid forests below . It can be found in either unflooded humid forests or in várzea forests, which are seasonally flooded. It is non-migratory

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: amazonica of the Rio Madeira stipplethroat is found in south-central Amazonian Brazil from the watershed of the Juruá River east to the Madeira River, in Peru's Department of Madre de Dios, and in far northern Bolivia's Pando Department.Clements, J

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: haematonota of the rufous-backed stipplethroat is found in eastern Peru south of the Napo and Amazon rivers between the departments of Loreto and Madre de Dios and in the adjacent Brazilian states of Amazonas and Acre

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Selva cacique is endemic to eastern Peru where it is found in the regions of Ucayali, Cusco and Madre de Dios, and the adjoining Acre State in northwestern Brazil

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: australis: from Cusco and Madre de Dios departments in southern Peru south into northwestern Bolivia as far as Cochabamba Department In most of its range the stripe-chested antwren inhabits the edges of humid evergreen forest and also secondary forest and bamboo stands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: leucophrys: eastern Andes from southern Peru's Cuzco and Madre de Dios departments south into northern Argentina as far as La Rioja Province The white-throated tyrannulet inhabits a variety of temperate landscapes in the Andes and other mountains

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It occurs in southeastern Peru from eastern Cuzco Department through Puno Department and possibly Madre de Dios Department into western Bolivia where its range continues through La Paz Department into Cochabamba Department
